subject: Gaming
title: Gaming Disorder: When Play Becomes a Problem
description: A quick, practical guide to Gaming Disorder: When Play Becomes a Problem.

hook.text: The WHO classified gaming disorder as an official condition in 2018. Not everyone agreed with the decision.

buildup.text: Gaming disorder means impaired control over gaming, increasing priority over other activities.

discovery.text: Only 1-3% of gamers meet the clinical criteria. The vast majority play without any disorder.

twist.text: Many psychologists argued against the classification. They said it pathologizes a normal hobby.

climax.text: The debate continues: is gaming disorder a real condition or a symptom of other problems like depression?

punchline.text: Only 1-3% qualify. The diagnosis is real but rare and debated.

quiz.question: What percentage of gamers meet clinical criteria for gaming disorder?
